channel.title: Comments for page "Clown Breeding 101 with Prof. Richard C. Normus"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Clown Breeding 101 with Prof. Richard C. Normus"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy fuckin - this is the best thing I've read in two years. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have similar feelings on this, but I find some parts of it funny, and that saves it from a downvote.

The horror and mystery of 2912, as stated before, was how fucked up and weird it got, especially the 'child breaker' part of the article. This, on the other hand, just outright removes the horror by telling us. And while for some that works better, but for me, my mind wandered for what exactly they did to these kids, and that's why it worked.

Not to say that nothing worked here. Far from it, I thought some parts were funny, but not enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last line is comedy gold. Everything else feels like a caricature of a caricature of a caricature, so far removed from any sense of actual character that it feels like you're writing a parody of the group. Aside from that, 2912 also worked in part because it didn't show all its cards and played up the horror angle by having being subtle while still maintaining an "in your face" vibe with its whole deal. You play this for laughs, but it loses everything I liked about that article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While you have thankfully toned down on the unlikeability of the speaker,1 the content is not that interesting.

With 2912, the horror of clown breeding is implied and subtle. Here, you essentially stated that which were implied in 2912 and added stuff like selective breeding. Perhaps it is suitable, but not groundbreaking.

Footnotes
1. I guess this works better than using every bigoted trope out there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me why I love The Circus. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I…think I'm missing a joke here, but by my own admitted writing standards, this gets my +1.

Well written, if…odd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is vulgar and juvenile and I liked it. +1
